title: Glenohumeral (Shoulder) Joint – Coronal Section
description: Coronal section of the glenohumeral joint from Gray's Anatomy showing the ball-and-socket articulation between the humeral head and the glenoid cavity of the scapula, reinforced by the glenoid labrum, joint capsule, and supraspinatus tendon passing beneath the acromion.
clinical_notes: The subacromial space (between supraspinatus and acromion) is the site of impingement syndrome. OT relevance: shoulder range of motion assessment, ADL adaptations for limited shoulder abduction, positioning after rotator cuff repair.
